idea-plugin.name: ChainlessChain IDE Bridge
idea-plugin.description:
ChainlessChain IDE Bridge brings the ChainlessChain cc agent CLI into your JetBrains IDE — a full AI coding agent in a side panel that sees your editor, edits through native diffs you approve, and runs your app, without leaving the IDE.

It runs a localhost MCP server exposing your active selection, diagnostics, open editors and native diff review as tools the agent can call, and writes a discovery lockfile so a cc agent you run in any terminal auto-connects to this project's bridge.

What you get
  • Chat tool window with conversation tabs — talk to the agent in the right-dock "ChainlessChain" panel: streaming replies, tool-call trace, and multiple conversations as tabs (each its own cc agent process; switch instantly, close, reopen). Tabs and their sessions resume across IDE restarts. New chat (Ctrl/Cmd+Alt+Shift+D), reopen last closed (Ctrl/Cmd+Alt+Shift+R).
  • Editor-aware out of the box — your selection and open tabs ride along with every prompt; after the agent edits a file, the IDE's fresh errors/warnings flow back into the same run so it fixes what it broke. (CC_IDE_CONTEXT=0 to opt out.)
  • Native diff review — file edits become a side-by-side IDE diff: Accept, Request changes… (inline notes go back to the agent), or Reject (never writes). Multi-file changes open as a single openMultiDiff batch — accept all, pick files, or reject.
  • Approval modes & extended thinking — slash commands in the panel: /auto (accept edits), /bypass, /normal; /think · /ultrathink · /think-off (Anthropic). Also /new /stop /cost /context /plan /approve /reject.
  • Interactive plan & approval cards — plans and tool-permission prompts render as cards with Approve / Deny (Approve / Reject) buttons, right in the chat.
  • Selection actions & @-mentions — right-click code → Explain or Refactor; Ctrl/Cmd+Alt+Shift+K inserts an @file#L5-10 reference for the selection; type @ for a completion popup of @selection/@diagnostics + project files + class & method symbols.
  • In-editor app preview — Start App Preview finds your dev script, runs the dev server, detects the URL and embeds it (JCEF, with an external-browser fallback); a one-click Restart if the server dies.
  • Live context window — a subtle line under the chat shows model context usage after each turn.
Requirements: the ChainlessChain cc CLI on your PATH (npm i -g chainlesschain) and a configured model (Tools → "ChainlessChain: Configure LLM", or cc config). Open-source: github.com/chainlesschain/chainlesschain.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.4.29 / 0.4.30 — fix: "Configure LLM" now pre-fills your config — no more re-typing model+key. The wizard pre-fills your current model, base URL and vision model, pre-selects your current provider (✓ 当前), and lets you leave the API key blank to keep the stored one (only required when there's no key yet or you switch providers). The config itself was never lost — it lives in ~/.chainlesschain/config.json and survives CLI updates; the wizard just didn't pre-fill it. (Mirrors the VS Code 0.34.0 fix. 0.4.30 adds this very change-note, which 0.4.29 omitted.)

0.4.28 — docs: the Marketplace "What's New" now lists every release (it had silently stopped at 0.4.19 — change-notes are no longer missed).

0.4.27 — cleared the 2 deprecated-API usages the Marketplace verifier flagged (now URI.toURL() + a combo-box dialog), so the plugin stays compatible with future IntelliJ releases.

0.4.26 — fix: no more false "未检测到 cc CLI" when cc IS installed. The version probe timeout went 5s → 12s (cold starts), and since cc is also the C compiler's name, the plugin now resolves the binary with fallback (cc → chainlesschain → clc → clchain) and uses it for the chat spawn and every probe.

0.4.25 — the agent can actually ask you a question: ask_user_question now pops a dialog (single-choice / multi-select checkboxes / free-text input) so the agent can clarify instead of guessing. Requires cc ≥ 0.162.95.

0.4.24 — fix: a graceful "couldn't ask in the panel" degradation no longer renders as a scary ✗ ask_user_question failure.

0.4.23 — new ⚙ LLM → 检查 cc 更新… menu item (check on demand), plus a clear hint when the cc CLI isn't installed.

0.4.22 — you're now told when a newer cc CLI is published on npm, so you don't silently miss new features.

0.4.21 — fix: reconfiguring the LLM now restarts the running chat so it picks up the new provider/model/key (no more "配置完还没用 / 新开对话才行").

0.4.20 — new /review command: review your uncommitted git diff right from the chat (matches the VS Code panel).

0.4.19 — fix: the suggested vision model now matches the CLI's actual default (doubao-seed-2-0-lite-260215) instead of an older snapshot, so the prefilled value equals what cc agent --image uses.

0.4.18 — dedicated image-recognition (vision) model entry: the ⚙ LLM button is now a small menu (configure full LLM or just the vision model), plus a Tools → Configure Vision Model action. Set llm.visionModel without re-running the whole wizard or re-typing your API key; blank reverts to the text model.

0.4.17 — first-run nudge: when no LLM provider is configured yet, the chat panel now shows a dim hint pointing at the ⚙ LLM button instead of staying blank until the first turn fails with a 401 (VS Code onboarding parity).

0.4.16 — chat panel LLM UX: (1) paste screenshots — Ctrl/Cmd+V an image to attach it to your next message (vision); (2) a ⚙ LLM quick-entry button by Send opens the provider/model wizard, and auth failures (401/403/missing key) now hint at it; (3) the wizard can set a separate image-recognition (vision) model — text and vision models no longer have to be the same.

0.4.15 — multi-line chat composer: the input now wraps across lines — Enter sends, Shift+Enter adds a newline (VS Code parity).

0.4.14 — extended-thinking reasoning (with /think) now streams into the transcript dimmed/italic, set apart from the answer (VS Code parity).

0.4.13 — Markdown rendering in the chat transcript: fenced/inline code (monospace) and bold now render instead of raw markers (VS Code parity). Streaming stays plain, then snaps to styled.

0.4.12 — "Fix with ChainlessChain" intention: on an error/warning line, Alt+Enter offers a quick-fix that seeds the chat with a fix request scoped to that file + those problems (VS Code parity).

0.4.11 — slash-command autocomplete: typing / at the start of the chat input pops a chooser of the panel's commands (/new, /compact, /think, …), filtered as you type (VS Code parity).

0.4.10 — fix: wrap the diff-review document reads (openDiff/openMultiDiff) in a read action. Since 2026.2 the EDT no longer grants implicit read access, so building the diff threw "Read access is allowed from inside read-action only" on 2026.2+. No behavior change on 2025.x.

0.4.9 — fix: the chat tool window now deterministically uses the LLM provider you configured (cc config / Configure LLM), reading ~/.chainlesschain/config.json and passing it explicitly to cc agent — no more spurious Anthropic error: 401 when another provider is configured.

0.4.8 — /compact in the chat tool window (Claude-Code IDE parity): trims the live conversation history between turns (no extra LLM call) and reports the tokens saved. Mirrors the VS Code 0.31.0 feature.

0.4.7 — cleared the remaining API-compatibility flags the Plugin Verifier reported on 2026.x: the @-mention list renderer no longer uses a scheduled-for-removal helper, and the deprecated terminal customizer was removed (a cc you run in a terminal still auto-connects via the bridge's discovery lockfile).

0.4.6 — newer-IDE compatibility: removed the last deprecated-API usages (terminal-process listener + provider-chooser dialog). Plugin Verifier reports no internal / scheduled-for-removal / deprecated API usage on 2025.2.

0.4.5 — reverted the experimental 0.4.4 getTerminalOutput tool: the terminal API it used is not stable across IDE versions and caused errors on some builds. Everything else is unchanged from 0.4.3.

0.4.3 — the @-mention symbol completion now covers methods/functions too (not just classes) — type a method name to reference the file where it's defined.

0.4.2 — the @-mention popup now includes project class symbols (type a class name to reference the file where it's defined), alongside @selection/@diagnostics and files.

0.4.1 — expanded the plugin description / what's-new (no functional change since 0.4.0).

0.4.0 — VS Code feature parity.
  • Multi-conversation tabs (one agent per tab, resume across restarts).
  • Approval modes (/auto·/bypass·/normal) and extended thinking (/think·/ultrathink·/think-off), plus /new /stop /cost /context /plan /approve /reject.
  • Interactive plan & approval cards (Approve/Deny buttons).
  • Editor Explain / Refactor actions, @file#L reference insertion (Ctrl/Cmd+Alt+K), and an @-mention popup.
  • Native diff "Request changes…" with inline notes, and batch openMultiDiff multi-file review.
  • In-editor app preview (JCEF) with Start/Stop.
  • Live context-window indicator; new-chat / reopen-closed shortcuts.

action.description: Show the IDE bridge MCP server status

action.text: ChainlessChain: Team Monitor (团队任务监控)
action.description: Watch a cc team run --state snapshot: task graph, lease holders, progress

action.text: ChainlessChain IDE: Diagnose Bridge (诊断桥接)
action.description: Show cc ide status / doctor / jetbrains — why a terminal cc agent did or didn't auto-connect

action.text: ChainlessChain IDE: Restart Bridge (重启桥接)
action.description: Restart the IDE bridge MCP server and rewrite its discovery lockfile

action.text: ChainlessChain: What's New (cc 更新说明)
action.description: Show the cc CLI's release notes (cc changelog --json, needs cc >= 0.162.151)
Guided LLM setup via cc config set + cc llm test

action.text: ChainlessChain: Configure Vision Model (配置图片识别模型)
action.description: Set just llm.visionModel — the image-recognition model used when you paste a screenshot
Open a new ChainlessChain chat conversation tab

action.text: ChainlessChain: Reopen Closed Chat (重开已关对话)
action.description: Reopen the most recently closed ChainlessChain conversation, resuming it

action.text: ChainlessChain: Explain Selection (解释选中代码)
action.description: Send the selected code to ChainlessChain chat and ask for an explanation

action.text: ChainlessChain: Refactor Selection (重构选中代码)
action.description: Send the selected code to ChainlessChain chat and ask for a refactor

action.text: ChainlessChain: Insert File Reference (@文件引用)
action.description: Insert an @file reference (with selected line range) into the ChainlessChain chat input

action.text: ChainlessChain: Generate Project Memory (生成项目记忆 cc.md)
action.description: Inventory the project into a cc.md that cc agent auto-loads (chainlesschain init)

action.text: ChainlessChain: Show Project Memory Files (查看项目记忆文件)
action.description: List the effective memory-file chain (chainlesschain memory files)

action.text: ChainlessChain: Start App Preview (启动应用预览)
action.description: Run the project's dev script and show the app in the preview tool window

action.text: ChainlessChain: Stop App Preview (停止应用预览)
action.description: Stop the App Preview dev server
